desc_short: Befriend the monster. Protect the world.
desc_long: The Monster Rangers are a frontier order dedicated to the belief that monsters are not aberrations, but living parts of Obscuria's ecology. Founded in 1903 by Baron Davis, the Rangers exist to study monsters without exploitation, protect them from eradication, and defend human communities from panic-driven destruction. They walk the line between trust and danger — forming bonds where possible, standing firm where necessary, and choosing understanding over extermination.
history: The Monster Rangers were formally established in 1903 by Baron Davis after witnessing repeated frontier massacres — both of monsters and of settlements — caused by ignorance, rumor, and reckless Thyr pursuit. Drawing from scouts, naturalists, folklorists, and survivors, the early Rangers cataloged monsters, mapped territories, and proved that many creatures could be reasoned with, relocated, or defended rather than destroyed.

core_values: Believe monsters are living beings, not targets
Study monsters to prevent fear-driven violence
Protect monsters, people, and places from exploitation
Befriend when possible, intervene when necessary
what_they_fight_for: Peaceful coexistence between monsters and settlements
Accurate knowledge of monster behavior and ecology
Protection of monster habitats and migration routes
A future where monsters are understood, not hunted
what_they_fight_against: Exploitation of monsters for profit, trophies, or fuel
Reckless Thyr extraction that destabilizes ecosystems
Those who profit from turning monsters into monsters

reputation.allies_see_them_as: Level-headed protectors, expert trackers, and the first line of calm when monsters appear
reputation.enemies_see_them_as: Monster lovers, obstructionists, and meddlers standing in the way of easy profit
reputation.monsters_see_them_as: Humans who do not always attack — sometimes helpers, sometimes wardens
reputation.the_canyon_sees_them_as: Watchers. Walkers of old paths. Not conquerors.

faction_mechanics.signature_ability_description: Monster Rangers can befriend monsters using Pocket Snacks (contested Quality rolls) or specialized befriending abilities. Befriended monsters activate with Rangers, turning enemies into allies.
faction_mechanics.playstyle: Adaptive specialists with incredible diversity. Monster Rangers field stealth scouts, mystic casters, medics, mechanics, psychics, undead caretakers, titan specialists, and flying daredevils. They excel at unconventional solutions, terrain control, and turning the battlefield's monsters against their opponents.
strengths: Extreme unit diversity and specialist options
Befriend ability turns monsters into allies
Strong in hazardous and mystical terrain
Excellent at scenario objectives requiring finesse
Relics and mystical powers provide game-changing moments
weaknesses: Dependent on terrain and monster presence

preferred_states.reason: Rangers' ideal: controlled but not destroyed, monsters contained not erased, land stable but alive.
Rangers thrive in feral biomes, can navigate and befriend wild beasts, understand the growth.
Rangers have gas masks and respirators, know how to survive the storms, can operate where others suffocate.

neutral_states.reason: Skull Rangers and Psychics excel here, but most Rangers find Bloodless difficult to manage.
Mystical expertise helps, but Crow Queen's control makes befriending dangerous.
Not ideal, but Rangers can operate in Shine Rider territory when needed.

opposed_states.reason: Liberty Corps suppresses monsters and mysticism — nothing for Rangers to work with.
Monsterology's scarred wastelands offer only mutilated specimens and exploitation.
Automat dominance, toxic machinery, no living creatures — Rangers struggle without monsters to befriend.

faction_features.name: Befriend Monsters
faction_features.effect: Monster Rangers can befriend monsters using Pocket Snacks (contested Quality rolls) or specialized befriending abilities (befriend_beast, befriend_bloodless, befriend_spirits, befriend_automat, befriend_kaiju). Befriended monsters activate with Rangers.

faction_features.name: Branch System
faction_features.effect: Rangers belong to different Branches (Crow, Conjure Guard, Doom Mime, Signal, Skull, Yokai, etc.) which grant thematic abilities and playstyles.

faction_features.name: Diverse Specialists
faction_features.effect: Monster Rangers field incredible variety: stealth scouts, mystic casters, medics, mechanics, psychics, undead caretakers, titan specialists, flying daredevils, and more.

weapons_note: Unit costs include standard-issue weapons. Upgradeable weapons (like Harpoons) have additional point costs listed in optional_upgrades.

units.name: Core Ranger
units.lore: Field-trained Monster Ranger infantry straight from Boot Camp. Signature tool: hatchet, equal parts weapon and working tool.
units.tactics: Baseline skirmisher. Thrown hatchets for short-range pressure, finish in melee. Use in numbers to screen more expensive units and contest objectives. Witch-Stitched is a cheap upgrade that meaningfully improves survivability. Pocket Snacks opens Befriend plays that Core Rangers wouldn't otherwise attempt.

optional_upgrades.name: Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Coffin Cough and Blinding effects.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A heavy-duty respirator built from salvaged canyon parts and rubber tubing. The wearer is completely immune to Coffin Cough — the toxic spore clouds that drift through the canyon lowlands — and cannot be Blinded by smoke, dust, or chemical effects. Standard kit for Rangers operating near the Brine Flats or the lower Coffin vents.

optional_upgrades.name: Witch-Stitched
optional_upgrades.desc_short: +1 Defense. Light Armor.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: Armor reinforced with hex-thread and warded leather panels sewn by a Dark Librarian or Conjure Guard. Adds +1 to the unit's Defense roll, representing a light physical barrier hardened by protective stitching. Unlike heavy plate, Witch-Stitched armor does not slow movement — Rangers can still climb, crawl, and sprint freely.

optional_upgrades.name: Pocket Snacks
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game, gain advantage on a Befriend attempt.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A pouch of dried monster-friendly treats — sun-cured jerky, sugar-cured beetles, honeyed bones. Once per game, the Ranger may produce a snack when attempting to Befriend a Beast or Creature, gaining Advantage on that roll. A small thing that goes a long way in the canyon.

units.name: Cargo Tiger Porter
units.lore: Hard-working employees running Cargo Tiger routes and delivering packages across the canyon.
units.tactics: Backline logistics and objective play. Load_and_carry lets them move extracted resources or objectives without speed penalty. Cut_and_run keeps them alive when targeted. Extraction Priority rewards getting objectives off the table — prioritize their survival when they are carrying something valuable.

optional_upgrades.name: Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Coffin Cough and Blinding effects.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A heavy-duty respirator built from salvaged canyon parts and rubber tubing. The wearer is completely immune to Coffin Cough — the toxic spore clouds that drift through the canyon lowlands — and cannot be Blinded by smoke, dust, or chemical effects. Standard kit for Rangers operating near the Brine Flats or the lower Coffin vents.

Unlike heavy plate, Witch-Stitched armor does not slow movement — Rangers can still climb, crawl, and sprint freely.

optional_upgrades.desc_long: A pouch of dried monster-friendly treats — sun-cured jerky, sugar-cured beetles, honeyed bones. Once per game, the Ranger may produce a snack when attempting to Befriend a Beast or Creature, gaining Advantage on that roll. A small thing that goes a long way in the canyon.

units.name: Crow Ranger
units.lore: First Ranger Branch. Masters of stealth and speed. If you can see them, something has already gone wrong.
units.tactics: Fast scout and infiltrator. Use Stealth to close distance without drawing fire, then strike isolated targets. Climber lets them hold elevated positions that most units cannot contest. Witch-Stitched is a meaningful upgrade — D2 is survivable but D3 is comfortable.

optional_upgrades.name: Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Coffin Cough and Blinding effects.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A heavy-duty respirator built from salvaged canyon parts and rubber tubing. The wearer is completely immune to Coffin Cough — the toxic spore clouds that drift through the canyon lowlands — and cannot be Blinded by smoke, dust, or chemical effects. units.name: Clocktalker Mechanic
units.lore: Monster Ranger mechanics and Mechan tamers. They speak to machines the way other Rangers speak to beasts.
units.tactics: Support and construct control. Repair keeps vehicles and devices in the fight. Befriend Automat can neutralize or redirect enemy mechanical units — an underrated play in scenarios with constructs. Tough gives them a meaningful chance of surviving the melee their wrench invites.

optional_upgrades.name: Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Coffin Cough and Blinding effects.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A heavy-duty respirator built from salvaged canyon parts and rubber tubing. optional_upgrades.desc_long: A pouch of dried monster-friendly treats — sun-cured jerky, sugar-cured beetles, honeyed bones. Once per game, the Ranger may produce a snack when attempting to Befriend a Beast or Creature, gaining Advantage on that roll. A small thing that goes a long way in the canyon.

units.name: Relic Guardian
units.lore: Field officers entrusted with sacred Conjure Guard relics. The relic chooses the bearer as much as the bearer chooses the relic.
units.tactics: Hero unit. The relic is the whole point — timing it correctly defines your game. Most relics are once per game, so hold them until the moment is right rather than firing them early. Roluun's Boots is the odd one out: passive Move bonus that stacks well with aggressive positioning. Witch-Stitched brings this unit to D4, which is very comfortable for a frontline Hero.

optional_upgrades.name: Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Coffin Cough and Blinding effects.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A heavy-duty respirator built from salvaged canyon parts and rubber tubing. The wearer is completely immune to Coffin Cough — the toxic spore clouds that drift through the canyon lowlands — and cannot be Blinded by smoke, dust, or chemical effects. supplemental_abilities.name: Groom's Grail
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Once per game: 12" cone freeze for 2 rounds.

supplemental_abilities.name: Alune's Shovel
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Once per game: Create 10" Hazardous thorn wall.

supplemental_abilities.name: Blink Crown
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Once per game: Swap positions with any creature.

supplemental_abilities.name: Gravewarden Bell
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Once per game: 8" morale shock (Shaken → Panicked).

supplemental_abilities.name: Saltbound Reliquary
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Once per game: 6" anti-undead damage aura.

supplemental_abilities.name: Widow's Lantern
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Once per game: Remove Stealth and cover in 10" aura.

supplemental_abilities.name: Iron Psalm Nails
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Once per game: Anchor enemy for 2 rounds.

supplemental_abilities.name: Thurible of Crawling Smoke
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Once per game: Create 5" smoke cloud.

supplemental_abilities.name: Pact Mirror
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Once per game: Redirect all damage to ally.

units.name: Conjure Guard
units.lore: Mystic protectors who focus on Witch Stitches — systematic warding using thread and needle. Understand we are deeply linked like thread.
units.tactics: Position centrally to project Shield Aura (+1 Defense) across as many allies as possible. Hex debuffs the most dangerous enemy each round. Fire Hat is a complete role swap — take it if you need an offensive caster rather than a support anchor. Dark Librarian amplifies this unit significantly; they work well as a pair.

optional_upgrades.name: Fire Hat
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Replace shield_aura and hex with blast and fear. Become an offensive fire caster.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A volatile pyromancer's cap crammed with incendiary hexes. Replaces this unit's standard protective abilities (Shield Aura and Hex) with Blast and Fear — transforming them from a defensive support unit into an aggressive fire-caster. Powerful, but burns away the unit's protective role entirely. Use when you need offense, not cover.

optional_upgrades.name: Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Coffin Cough and Blinding effects.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A heavy-duty respirator built from salvaged canyon parts and rubber tubing. The wearer is completely immune to Coffin Cough — the toxic spore clouds that drift through the canyon lowlands — and cannot be Blinded by smoke, dust, or chemical effects. Standard kit for Rangers operating near the Brine Flats or the lower Coffin vents.

optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game, gain advantage on a Befriend attempt.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A pouch of dried monster-friendly treats — sun-cured jerky, sugar-cured beetles, honeyed bones. Once per game, the Ranger may produce a snack when attempting to Befriend a Beast or Creature, gaining Advantage on that roll. A small thing that goes a long way in the canyon.

units.name: Dark Librarian
units.lore: Arcane repositories of legends, stories, and Formulae. They study, catalog, and protect Monster Ranger knowledge. Motto: 'You are what you know.'
units.tactics: Backline support. Purification Ritual clears hazardous terrain once per game — save it for the right moment. Forbidden Knowledge boosts Befriend attempts for your whole force against a chosen monster type; declare this at game start based on what you expect to face. Formulae Master extends Conjure Guard range significantly — keeping them within 12" of each other is a meaningful force multiplier.

optional_upgrades.name: Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Coffin Cough and Blinding effects.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A heavy-duty respirator built from salvaged canyon parts and rubber tubing. The wearer is completely immune to Coffin Cough — the toxic spore clouds that drift through the canyon lowlands — and cannot be Blinded by smoke, dust, or chemical effects. Standard kit for Rangers operating near the Brine Flats or the lower Coffin vents.

optional_upgrades.desc_short: +1 Defense. Does not inhibit movement.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: Armor reinforced with hex-thread and warded leather panels sewn by a Dark Librarian or Conjure Guard. Adds +1 to the unit's Defense roll, representing a light physical barrier hardened by protective stitching. Unlike heavy plate, Witch-Stitched armor does not slow movement — Rangers can still climb, crawl, and sprint freely.

optional_upgrades.name: Pocket Snacks
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game, gain advantage on a Befriend attempt.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A pouch of dried monster-friendly treats — sun-cured jerky, sugar-cured beetles, honeyed bones. Once per game, the Ranger may produce a snack when attempting to Befriend a Beast or Creature, gaining Advantage on that roll. A small thing that goes a long way in the canyon.

units.name: Monster Medic
units.lore: Treat Monster and Manling alike. Med Kits contain as many poisons as medicines. They brew VitaGood, perform blood transfusions, and wear field Plague Doctor masks.
units.tactics: Each activation choose one: Blood Transfusion (heal an ally at cost to self — risky on low-Quality targets) or Venom (6" poison on an enemy). VitaGood tokens provide burst healing or emergency recovery. The gas mask is built in — put them in the worst terrain on the board and let them work. Witch-Stitched is a strong upgrade; D3 lets them actually survive the front line they keep walking into.

optional_upgrades.name: Witch-Stitched
optional_upgrades.desc_short: +1 Defense. Does not inhibit movement.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: Armor reinforced with hex-thread and warded leather panels sewn by a Dark Librarian or Conjure Guard. Adds +1 to the unit's Defense roll, representing a light physical barrier hardened by protective stitching. Unlike heavy plate, Witch-Stitched armor does not slow movement — Rangers can still climb, crawl, and sprint freely.

units.name: Psychic Ranger
units.lore: Pierce the BEYOND with Third Eye. Create Fortuna Charms, project consciousness to control minds. Rare and vital. Motto: 'Seeing is Believing.'
units.tactics: Support specialist with three distinct tools. Fortuna Charms provide reroll tokens — hand them out early and let your force use them on critical rolls. Third Eye reveals Stealth and hidden units, which is extremely valuable against Crow Rangers or Canyon Jackals. Mind Control on a successful 2+ successes gives you a free activation with an enemy unit — save it for their most dangerous piece. Befriend Spirits opens Haunt plays in Haunted canyon states.

optional_upgrades.name: Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Coffin Cough and Blinding effects.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A heavy-duty respirator built from salvaged canyon parts and rubber tubing. The wearer is completely immune to Coffin Cough — the toxic spore clouds that drift through the canyon lowlands — and cannot be Blinded by smoke, dust, or chemical effects. Standard kit for Rangers operating near the Brine Flats or the lower Coffin vents.

optional_upgrades.desc_short: +1 Defense. Does not inhibit movement.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: Armor reinforced with hex-thread and warded leather panels sewn by a Dark Librarian or Conjure Guard. Adds +1 to the unit's Defense roll, representing a light physical barrier hardened by protective stitching. Unlike heavy plate, Witch-Stitched armor does not slow movement — Rangers can still climb, crawl, and sprint freely.

optional_upgrades.name: Pocket Snacks
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game, gain advantage on a Befriend Haunt attempt.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A pouch of dried monster-friendly treats — sun-cured jerky, sugar-cured beetles, honeyed bones. Once per game, the Ranger may produce a snack when attempting to Befriend a Beast or Creature, gaining Advantage on that roll. A small thing that goes a long way in the canyon.

units.name: Yokai Ranger
units.lore: Specialists in Yokai and Kaiju. The branch started in 1909 when Baron Davis visited Osaka. Over 5000 Yokai Rangers operate in Japan. Regimented, scholarly, and extraordinarily calm when something very large is coming toward them.
units.tactics: Titan specialist. Befriend Kaiju with +2 bonus dice — Q3 becomes 5 dice against a Q6 Titan, which is a real chance. Kaiju Calming Ritual takes 2 turns but rewards +3 VP on success and removes a major threat from the board. Yokai Knowledge extends the bonus to nearby allies — keep them within 6" of anything fighting a Titan.

optional_upgrades.name: Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to Coffin Cough and Blinding effects.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A heavy-duty respirator built from salvaged canyon parts and rubber tubing. The wearer is completely immune to Coffin Cough — the toxic spore clouds that drift through the canyon lowlands — and cannot be Blinded by smoke, dust, or chemical effects. Standard kit for Rangers operating near the Brine Flats or the lower Coffin vents.

optional_upgrades.name: Witch-Stitched
optional_upgrades.desc_short: +1 Defense. Does not inhibit movement.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: Armor reinforced with hex-thread and warded leather panels sewn by a Dark Librarian or Conjure Guard. Adds +1 to the unit's Defense roll, representing a light physical barrier hardened by protective stitching. Unlike heavy plate, Witch-Stitched armor does not slow movement — Rangers can still climb, crawl, and sprint freely.

optional_upgrades.name: Pocket Snacks
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game, gain advantage on a Befriend attempt.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A pouch of dried monster-friendly treats — sun-cured jerky, sugar-cured beetles, honeyed bones. Once per game, the Ranger may produce a snack when attempting to Befriend a Beast or Creature, gaining Advantage on that roll. A small thing that goes a long way in the canyon.

units.name: Scareforce Rattlekite
units.lore: Blappo invention — canvas wings, firework propulsion, daredevil pilots. They only land... or crash. Erratic flight makes them hard to hit. Glorious death trap.
units.tactics: High-speed bomber. Fly over enemy formations and drop flechettes for automatic hits. Must move 6"+ each activation or risk a crash — plan movement paths before the round starts. Witch-Stitched improves both survivability and the Barely Controlled threshold, which is a significant quality-of-life upgrade. Firework Bombs is a once-per-game Blast+Fear shot that can swing a morale cluster fight entirely.

optional_upgrades.name: Witch-Stitched
optional_upgrades.desc_short: +1 Defense. Barely Controlled test succeeds on 3+ instead of 4+.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: Armor reinforced with hex-thread and warded leather panels sewn by a Dark Librarian or Conjure Guard. Adds +1 to the unit's Defense roll, representing a light physical barrier hardened by protective stitching. Unlike heavy plate, Witch-Stitched armor does not slow movement — Rangers can still climb, crawl, and sprint freely.

optional_upgrades.name: Firework Bombs
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game: fire a firework bomb with blast and fear.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A brace of hand-packed canyon fireworks, loaded with flash powder and hex-shrapnel. Once per game, the unit may hurl a firework bomb that triggers both Blast (hitting all units in a radius) and Fear (forcing morale checks on survivors). Unpredictable, loud, and extremely satisfying.

optional_upgrades.name: Pocket Snacks
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game, gain advantage on a Befriend attempt with flying monsters.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A pouch of dried monster-friendly treats — sun-cured jerky, sugar-cured beetles, honeyed bones. Once per game, the Ranger may produce a snack when attempting to Befriend a Beast or Creature, gaining Advantage on that roll. A small thing that goes a long way in the canyon.

units.name: Doom Mime
units.lore: Secret police of Monster Rangers. Masked enforcers. Fixers and advisors who 'deal with' problems. Invite-only. Know 100 ways to make you cry with steel batons. Metal Mask built in.
units.tactics: Pressure engine. Stealth plus Fear creates board denial before the Mime commits to a fight — enemies give ground rather than trigger it. Once engaged it grinds things down with Brutal Blow. Silent prevents audio-based abilities from working nearby, which disrupts Signal Rangers, Riot Harmonists, and any unit relying on Inspire. Witch-Stitched brings it to D4, which is where a Brute wants to be.

optional_upgrades.name: Witch-Stitched
optional_upgrades.desc_short: +1 Defense. Does not inhibit movement.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: Armor reinforced with hex-thread and warded cloth panels sewn by a Conjure Guard. Adds +1 to the unit's Defense roll, representing a light physical barrier hardened by protective stitching. Unlike heavy plate, Witch-Stitched armor does not slow movement — Rangers can still climb, crawl, and sprint freely.

units.name: Brine Diver
units.lore: Deep sea divers in heavy pressure suits. The suit is their temple — rarely removed. Sea Rangers adapted to deep pressure. Electro-tridents crackle with Pierce energy.
units.tactics: Front-line tank. D5 plus Tougher means damage rolls against this unit often fail entirely. Pierce cuts through armored targets. Move 4" is slow but you do not need speed when you are this hard to remove — park on an objective and let enemies spend their activations trying to shift you. Harpoons extend reach to 14" total threat range, which changes how enemies approach the objective.

optional_upgrades.name: Harpoons
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain Ranged 10" attack. 14" total threat range (4" move + 10" range).
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A heavy mechanical harpoon rig strapped to the unit's back or forearm. Grants a Ranged attack with a 10" range, extending the unit's total threat range to 14" (4" move + 10" range). Effective against large Kaiju or pinning down fleeing enemies. The harpoon takes time to reload — use it wisely.

units.name: Signal Ranger
units.lore: Leaders, communicators and flag-bearers. Lead Calamity Parades and use Radio technology. Some jam Monsterology Dynamo equipment.
units.tactics: Standard leader with two exceptional once-per-game tools. Calamity Parade is a round-winning ability — save it for the activation where you need to cross the board, break a line, or reach a Titan before it does damage. Radiomancer Jam at 24" range can silently disable Jägers and Hexans without any fight — an invisible handicap on Monsterology's most dangerous pair. Gas Mask is cheap insurance against Chemist Lackeys.

optional_upgrades.name: Gas Mask
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Immune to environmental penalties.
optional_upgrades.desc_long: A heavy-duty respirator built from salvaged canyon parts and rubber tubing. The wearer is completely immune to Coffin Cough — the toxic spore clouds that drift through the canyon lowlands — and cannot be Blinded by smoke, dust, or chemical effects. Standard kit for Rangers operating near the Brine Flats or the lower Coffin vents.
